intro:
Around 327 stations are within a 30-minute commute of Oshiage, expanding to 718 stations within 45 minutes. Average rent within the 45-minute commute zone is around ¥93,600. Served by four lines — the Tokyo Metro Hanzomon Line, Toei Asakusa Line, Keisei Oshiage Line, and Tobu Skytree Line — Oshiage forms a hub that stretches from the area directly connected to Tokyo Skytree out toward Chiba and Saitama and as far as central Kanagawa Prefecture.

Redevelopment in Oshiage took off with the opening of Tokyo Skytree and Tokyo Solamachi, creating a distinctive district where tourism, commerce, and business functions are integrated. Skytree-related companies, media, tourism businesses, local small and midsize firms, and manufacturing workshops cluster here, and IT services and coworking spaces have been increasing in recent years. Commuters range widely from their 20s to their 50s, and the diversity of industries is a defining feature.

Through services between the Hanzomon Line and the Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line give direct access to Otemachi, Omotesando, Shibuya, Sangen-jaya, Futako-Tamagawa, and Tama-Plaza; through services between the Toei Asakusa Line and the Keikyu Main Line reach Shinagawa and Haneda Airport; the Keisei Oshiage Line and Keisei Main Line run toward Aoto and Keisei-Narita; and the Tobu Skytree Line reaches Kita-Senju, Kasukabe, and Shin-Koshigaya — all without transfers. The location combines airport access with multi-directional terminal functions.

If you want to keep rent down, realistic candidates include Yotsugi, Aoto, and Keisei-Tateishi on the Keisei Oshiage Line; Nishi-Arai, Umejima, and Shin-Koshigaya on the Tobu Skytree Line; Kita-Koshigaya and Kasukabe via Hanzomon Line through services onto the Tobu network; Asakusa, Kuramae, and Nishi-Magome on the Toei Asakusa Line; and Sumiyoshi and Kinshicho on the Tokyo Metro Hanzomon Line. For those who want to live close to work, the Narihirabashi, Hikifune, and Keisei-Hikifune areas are also popular.

| Area | Rent (single) | To Oshiage | Main lines |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Aoto | ¥70,000–90,000 | approx. 5–10 min | Keisei Oshiage Line, Keisei Main Line |
| Nishi-Arai | ¥70,000–90,000 | approx. 15–20 min | Tobu Skytree Line |
| Shin-Koshigaya | ¥70,000–90,000 | approx. 20–25 min | Tobu Skytree Line |
| Kinshicho | ¥80,000–100,000 | approx. 5–10 min | Tokyo Metro Hanzomon Line, JR Sobu Line |
| Asakusa | ¥80,000–100,000 | approx. 5–10 min | Toei Asakusa Line, Tokyo Metro Ginza Line |

On Kayoha, set Oshiage as your commute destination to compare 1831 stations on a color-coded commute-time map in 5-minute increments, and narrow down candidate areas with an AI recommendation of 20 stations at once, alongside rent estimates and neighborhood profiles.

faq.q: Which areas within a 30-minute commute of Oshiage have relatively affordable rent?
faq.a: Good benchmarks include Yotsugi, Aoto, and Keisei-Tateishi on the Keisei Oshiage Line; Nishi-Arai, Umejima, and Shin-Koshigaya on the Tobu Skytree Line; Kita-Koshigaya and Kasukabe via Tobu through services; Sumiyoshi on the Tokyo Metro Hanzomon Line; and Asakusa and Kuramae on the Toei Asakusa Line. Rent for singles mostly falls in the ¥70,000–90,000 range, with plenty of options.

faq.q: Which lines offer the shortest commutes to Oshiage?
faq.a: Four lines form the mainstay of the 5–20 minute zone: the Tokyo Metro Hanzomon Line (toward Kinshicho and Sumiyoshi), the Toei Asakusa Line (toward Honjo-Azumabashi and Asakusa), the Keisei Oshiage Line (toward Hikifune and Yahiro), and the Tobu Skytree Line (toward Tokyo Skytree Station and Hikifune). The Hanzomon Line's through service with the Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line and the Toei Asakusa Line's through service with Keikyu give the area a notably wide-reaching network.

faq.q: What is the Oshiage area like? Is it a good place to live?
faq.a: The area around Oshiage Station, centered on Tokyo Skytree and Tokyo Solamachi, blends tourism, commerce, and housing. The neighboring Narihirabashi, Hikifune, and Kyojima areas are residential districts with an old-town, shitamachi feel, making the area popular with singles, working couples, and young families who want both livability and easy access to central Tokyo.

faq.q: Which areas are popular with families commuting to Oshiage?
faq.a: Kita-Koshigaya, Kasukabe, and Sengendai on the Tobu Skytree Line, Tama-Plaza and Azamino via the Hanzomon Line's through service with the Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line, and Keisei-Yawata and Keisei-Funabashi on the Keisei Oshiage Line (Keisei Main Line) tend to offer good access to nurseries, parks, and supermarkets, making them popular with families. Commute times fall within roughly 20–45 minutes.

faq.q: Are there stations where trains originate, making it easier to get a seat when commuting to Oshiage?
faq.a: The Tobu Skytree Line has trains originating from the Kita-Kasukabe and Minami-Kurihashi side, the Hanzomon Line from the Chuo-Rinkan side, and the Toei Asakusa Line from Nishi-Magome and the Keikyu through-service side, which improves your chances of commuting seated. If you want to avoid crowds, it is worth considering areas near originating stations.
